Winter Dance Concert
Feb 25, 2026 - Mar 1, 2026
The Conservatory Dance Company presents a winter concert featuring a diverse range of ballet, modern, jazz, hip hop, and contemporary works choreographed by renowned guest artists and dance professionals. Winter Dance showcases work by Al Blackstone (Jazz), Roderick George (Contemporary), Dwight Rhoden (Ballet), and Kenichi Kasamatsu (Hip Hop).

Jason Isbell and the 400 Unit
Mar 2, 2026																											   | 8:00 pm - 1:00 pm
Six-time Grammy Award-winning singer/songwriter Jason Isbell is one of the most respected and celCebrated artists of his generation. The North Alabama native possesses an incredible penchant for identifying and articulating some of the deepest, yet simplest, human emotions, and turning them into beautiful poetry through song. Isbell sings of the everyday human condition with thoughtful, heartfelt, and sometimes brutal honesty.

The Blues Brothers
Mar 6, 2026																											   | 7:00 pm - 1:00 pm
Jake and Elwood Blues make their leap from the small screen of Saturday Night Live to the giant screen of the Rangos Giant Cinema in their first feature film, “The Blues Brothers.” This iconic film is a tale of redemption, family, and the magic of blues music – something you won’t want to miss out on with our 49-speaker surround sound system. “The Blues Brothers” kicks off the Kamin Science Centers' Cult Classic series on Fri., March 6 at 7 p.m.
